project.title: Protein expression and identification of Tyrp1 Mutant Variants Associated with OCA3 form of albinism.
project.projectDescription: Oculocutaneous albinism type 3 (OCA3) is an autosomal recessive disorder caused by mutations in the TYRP1 gene. Tyrosinase-related protein 1 (Tyrp1) is involved in eumelanin synthesis, catalyzing the oxidation of 5,6-dihydroxyindole-2-carboxylic acid oxidase (DHICA) to 5,6-indolequinone-2-carboxylic acid (IQCA). In our work, we are trying to understand the effect of genetic mutations at the level of protein atomic structure and establish a link between the effect of mutations and disease phenotype.  Here, for the first time, four OCA3-causing mutations of Tyrp1, C30R, H215Y, D308N, and R326H, were investigated computationally and experimentally to understand Tyrp1 protein stability and catalytic activity. Using the Tyrp1 crystal structure (PDB:5M8L), global mutagenesis was conducted to evaluate mutant protein stability. Consistent with our predictions the foldability parameter, C30R and H215Y should exhibit greater instability, and two other mutants, D308N and R326H, are expected to keep a native conformation. Mass-spectroscopy analysis has confirmed the identity of mutant recombinant variants. SDS-PAGE and Western blot analysis of the purified recombinant proteins confirmed that the foldability parameter correctly predicted the effect of mutations critical for protein stability. C18 data see C4 data.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Recombinant human wild-type intra-melanosomal domain of the tyrosinase-related protein (residues 25–537 of the native protein), Tyrp1, and OCA3-related mutants, C30R, H215Y, D308N, and R326H were expressed in baculovirus and produced in the whole insect T. ni larvae. Proteins were purified using the combination of affinity and gel-filtration chromatographies. Protein identity was confirmed by Western blot analysis using an anti-Tyrp1 antibody. To confirm the presence of the amino acid substitution in the working isolate we used bottom-up mass spectrometry analysis. Due to the properties of the expected variant peptides an easy spray C4 column was used while a C18 column was used for a WT vs. C30R variant. Details for the mass spec. analysis are found in the publication. The different identifiers are used to describe these two data sets.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MaxQuant 1.6.10.43 was used to analyze the resulting data searching against the common contaminants list and a list containing each of the variants. Lower FDR’s were used due to the low complexity of the sample and the internal controls available from the set of raw files.
